summary.intent: Review and address new comments on PR #479 in the moltis repo, fixing issues in the Dockerfile and MCP documentation, then commit and push the changes.
summary.outcome: Fixed two review threads: improved Docker layer caching by splitting a monolithic RUN command into independent layers, and clarified wording in mcp.md about Docker CLI installation vs. daemon socket mounting. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The moltis repo uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for PR branches
Node.js 22 is installed via NodeSource in the Dockerfile
The MCP setup uses a mounted Docker socket for daemon connectivity rather than a bundled daemon

code.finding: Monolithic RUN command was split into three independent layers (base packages, Node.js 22 via NodeSource, Docker CLI) to improve cache invalidation behavior — upgrading Node no longer invalidates unrelated layers
Wording was clarified to distinguish between Docker CLI (installed in the image) and the Docker daemon (provided via mounted socket)

workflow: gh CLI is used to fetch unresolved review threads from PRs
Review threads are resolved programmatically via Bash after applying fixes
Worktree branch was already clean and pushed before the new review comments were addressed
